name: Charlie Persip
abstract: Charles Lawrence Persip (July 26, 1929 – August 23, 2020), known as Charli Persip and formerly as Charlie Persip (he changed the spelling of his name to Charli in the late 1960s), was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz
WLIB-FM Festival of Music and Drama

works.t: Gimme a Pigfoot (And a Bottle of Beer)

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Newport Jazz Festival: Sam Rivers Quartet and Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: Here I’m playing bass with Charlie Persip who is one of my favorite big band drummers from Dizzy Gillespie’s big band.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: On that date we had Paul Chambers, Wynton Kelly, Charlie Persip, Gigi Gryce, Art Farmer, Shahib Shihab.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerome Richardson
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah. Jimmy Cleveland, Hank Jones, Kenny Burrell, Joe Benjamin and Charlie Persip on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerome Richardson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Called Where? with Eric Dolphy, Mal Waldron, Charlie Persip, and George Duvivier.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Melba Liston
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'I think you should write these things for an album for me.' And I wrote them... And he and probably Charli Persip – can’t think of the bass player now. It’s a nice album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: And it was Art Blakey, Elvin Jones, Max Roach, Mel Lewis, and Charlie Persip. And I was more like the boy wonder at that time. They put me up and I sat in and played
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]
